independent_operator_examples.name: ETIC
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Focused Luxembourg Sproochentest preparation offer with a visible paid prep route.

independent_operator_examples.name: Lux'Languages
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Small specialist Luxembourgish prep provider with public paid preparation positioning.

independent_operator_examples.name: LuxLessons
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Niche Luxembourgish prep provider visible in the same route market with public online preparation messaging.

independent_operator_examples.name: Prolingua
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Broad private school with overlapping Sproochentest and Luxembourgish offers, proving the market is not empty.

pricing_or_offer_proof: Observed public alternatives range from a free online tier to roughly 745-980 EUR paid preparation offers.
social_funnel_signal: Facebook group search surfaces a Luxembourgish-learning community layer (`Luxembourgish learning`, `Luxembourg Training, Education, Language Courses Luxembourg`, `Tandem Luxembourg`, `Luxembourgish coach/language exchange partner`). This supports top-of-funnel demand and peer exchange, but the OCR pass did not show a clear bundle-led paid funnel from those groups.
takeaway: The route is already monetized by several small paid providers, so demand is real and fragmented rather than institution-only.
why_not_stronger: Current evidence proves fragmented prep demand, but not bundle-specific willingness to pay or partner-led funnel behavior.

independent_operator_examples.name: LuxLessons
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Small Luxembourgish training provider already positioned publicly in the same local language and prep market.

independent_operator_examples.name: Allingua
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Commercial Luxembourg language provider with professional-language positioning relevant to healthcare workers.

independent_operator_examples.name: Prolingua
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Established private school covering overlapping workplace-language needs.

pricing_or_offer_proof: Current proof is mainly presence of paid Luxembourgish providers; no clean healthcare-specific public price signal was captured.
social_funnel_signal: Facebook group search shows the same Luxembourgish-learning community layer (`Luxembourgish learning`, `Tandem Luxembourg`, `Luxembourgish coach/language exchange partner`), but no healthcare-specific community or operator funnel surfaced. The social layer supports underlying demand, not the medical wedge by itself.
takeaway: There is real paid supply in adjacent Luxembourgish training, so the underlying problem is not hypothetical.
why_not_stronger: Current operator proof is adjacent Luxembourgish-route demand, not a clean healthcare-specific operator cluster.

independent_operator_examples.name: inlingua Luxembourg
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Broad private language school serving the same general business-French lane.

independent_operator_examples.name: INLL
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Institutional provider proving category demand, but not fragmented small-operator supply.

pricing_or_offer_proof: No clean small-operator price proof was captured in the validated evidence set.
social_funnel_signal: Facebook search for French-in-Luxembourg terms collapsed into general Luxembourg and expat groups rather than niche French-learning communities. This supports local newcomer demand, but not a dedicated small-operator funnel for business French.
takeaway: Commercial supply exists, but it is led by broad schools and institutions rather than fragmented small operators.
why_not_stronger: Current evidence shows category demand, but not a clear long tail of niche business-French operators.

takeaway: The general professional-French market is real, but the evidence is still broad-provider-led rather than operator-fragmented.
why_not_stronger: The sector-specific wedge is not yet backed by a visible set of niche operators with clear public offers.
